title: Advanced Modeling Capstone and Reproducible Research · Assumption Clinic
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving an independently reviewed applied research question. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reproducible capstone reproducibility computational study and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Formulate research question in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive computational experiment in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute verification validation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Reproducible Release applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Communicate reproducible release in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
